findings.description: yaml.load(f) uses the unsafe PyYAML loader path: a config file an attacker can influence can embed Python object tags (e.g. !!python/object/apply:os.system) leading to arbitrary code execution; use yaml.safe_load (CWE-502).

Precedence is inverted vs the docstring: env overlay is applied (line 67) before file values (line 68), so file contents override env vars, meaning an operator's env-set security control (flag/timeout) can be silently overridden by the config file; security-adjacent but primarily a logic bug for the logic critic.

config_path is caller-supplied and opened/read directly; if any call site ever passes externally-influenced input it becomes an arbitrary file read / traversal vector, but within this module's stated entry points the path is application-controlled, so low confidence.
